name: Scavenge
reminder_text: (If this would die, you may exile it instead. If you do, search your library for an artifact.)
flavor_connection: Explorers salvaging useful technology from fallen creatures and broken automatons in the dungeon depths.
design_rationale: Simple mechanic that creates value from creature death, supports artifact themes, and provides library filtering. Works well at common with clean reminder text.
common_patterns: Creature dies → exile instead → search for artifact with mana value 1 or less
Creature dies → exile instead → search for Equipment
uncommon_patterns: Scavenge with size restriction (mana value 2 or less)
Scavenge that puts artifact directly into play
Scavenge with additional bonus when triggered
rare_patterns: Scavenge that searches for specific powerful artifacts
Repeated scavenge effects or scavenge that triggers off other creatures

example_cards.name: Dungeon Scrounger
example_cards.oracle_text: Scavenge (If this would die, you may exile it instead. If you do, search your library for an artifact card with mana value 1 or less, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.)

example_cards.name: Tech Salvage Automaton
example_cards.oracle_text:
When Tech Salvage Automaton enters the battlefield, scavenge. (Search your library for an artifact card with mana value 2 or less, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.)
Scavenge

example_cards.name: Master Archaeologist
example_cards.oracle_text:
Whenever a creature you control with scavenge dies, you may pay {1}. If you do, that creature's scavenge ability triggers an additional time.
Scavenge

name: Descend
reminder_text: (This triggers if a permanent entered your graveyard from the battlefield this turn.)
flavor_connection: The deeper you venture into the dungeon, the more dangerous and rewarding it becomes. Loss fuels discovery.
design_rationale: Rewards incremental advantage and creates interesting sequencing decisions. Different from existing 'descend' by tracking any permanent, not just permanents in graveyard.
common_patterns: Descend — This creature gets +1/+1 until end of turn
uncommon_patterns: Descend — Return target card from graveyard to hand
Descend — Deal damage to any target
rare_patterns: Descend — Search library for specific card type
Descend — Multiple triggers with different thresholds

example_cards.name: Depth Walker
example_cards.oracle_text: Descend — Whenever Depth Walker attacks, if a permanent entered your graveyard from the battlefield this turn, defending player loses 2 life.

example_cards.name: Subsurface Oracle
example_cards.oracle_text: Descend — At the beginning of your end step, if a permanent entered your graveyard from the battlefield this turn, you may pay {2}. If you do, return target creature card from your graveyard to your hand.

example_cards.name: Anomalous Depths
example_cards.oracle_text:
Descend — At the beginning of your upkeep, if a permanent entered your graveyard from the battlefield last turn, draw a card.
Descend — Whenever a creature enters the battlefield under your control, if two or more permanents entered your graveyard from the battlefield this turn, put a +1/+1 counter on it.

name: Malfunction
reminder_text: (This permanent enters tapped. Remove a malfunction counter at the beginning of your upkeep.)
flavor_connection: Ancient technology is unreliable. Artifacts and constructs glitch, requiring time to recalibrate and function properly.
design_rationale: Balances powerful effects with timing cost. Creates deck-building tension between power level and consistency. Unique to artifact-heavy environment.
uncommon_patterns: Ways to remove malfunction counters faster
rare_patterns: High malfunction count with game-changing effects
Permanents that benefit from having malfunction counters

example_cards.name: Glitching Servo
example_cards.oracle_text:
Malfunction 1 (This permanent enters the battlefield tapped with a malfunction counter. Remove a malfunction counter at the beginning of your upkeep.)
Vigilance

example_cards.name: Unstable Power Core
example_cards.oracle_text:
Malfunction 2
{T}: Add {C}{C}{C}.
{1}, {T}: Deal 2 damage to any target.

example_cards.name: Ancient War Engine
example_cards.oracle_text:
Malfunction 3
Trample, vigilance, haste
Whenever Ancient War Engine attacks, it deals 2 damage to each opponent.

name: Augment
reminder_text: ({1}: Attach an Equipment you control to this creature. Augment only as a sorcery.)
flavor_connection: Survivors modify themselves with scavenged tech and bio-augmentations to survive the harsh post-apocalyptic world.
design_rationale: Simple creature-focused mechanic that creates Equipment synergies and interesting combat decisions. Easy to understand, works with existing cards.
common_patterns: When this creature becomes augmented, [small bonus]
uncommon_patterns: Bonuses that scale with number of equipments attached
rare_patterns: Augment that can attach any equipment from graveyard/library
Creatures that create equipment when augmented

example_cards.name: Frontier Scout
example_cards.oracle_text:
Augment ({1}: Attach an Equipment you control to this creature. Augment only as a sorcery.)
Whenever an Equipment becomes attached to Frontier Scout, it gets +1/+1 until end of turn.

example_cards.name: Cybernetic Warrior
example_cards.oracle_text:
Augment
As long as Cybernetic Warrior is equipped, it has first strike and gets +1/+0 for each Equipment attached to it.

example_cards.name: Master Augmenter
example_cards.oracle_text:
Augment costs {0} for creatures you control.
Whenever you augment a creature, create a Weapon token. (It's a colorless Equipment artifact with "Equipped creature gets +1/+0" and equip {1}.)

name: Overload
reminder_text: (To overload, exile the top three cards of your library. You may play them this turn.)
flavor_connection: Pushing ancient machinery beyond safe limits yields great power at the risk of catastrophic system failure and resource waste.
design_rationale: High-risk, high-reward mechanic for spells and activated abilities. Creates explosive turns and card advantage, but with real downside risk.
common_patterns: Overload 2 (exile two cards instead of three)
Simple overload effects on cheap spells
uncommon_patterns: Overload with additional restrictions or benefits
Overload that cares about specific card types exiled
rare_patterns: Overload effects that scale with cards exiled

example_cards.name: Power Surge
example_cards.oracle_text:
Power Surge deals 3 damage to any target.
Overload (To overload, exile the top three cards of your library. You may play them this turn.) — If you overload, Power Surge deals 5 damage instead.

example_cards.name: Unstable Researcher
example_cards.oracle_text:
{2}{U}, {T}: Draw a card.
{4}{U}, {T}: Draw two cards, then overload.

example_cards.name: Catastrophic Experiment
example_cards.oracle_text:
Overload, then overload again. (Exile the top six cards of your library. You may play them this turn.)
Deals damage to each creature equal to the number of cards exiled this way.

name: Jury-rig
reminder_text: ({2}: Attach target Equipment to this creature. It becomes an Aura with enchant creature.)
flavor_connection: Desperate survivors permanently fuse scavenged equipment to themselves in crude but effective bio-mechanical augmentations.
design_rationale: Transforms Equipment into permanent attachments, creating interesting sacrifice/risk decisions. Supports both Equipment and Enchantment themes.
uncommon_patterns: Jury-rig abilities that grant additional benefits
Creatures that get bigger based on jury-rigged equipment
rare_patterns: Creatures that create equipment to jury-rig

example_cards.name: Wasteland Scavenger
example_cards.oracle_text:
Jury-rig ({2}: Attach target Equipment to this creature. It becomes an Aura with enchant creature and loses its other abilities.)
Whenever an Equipment becomes jury-rigged to Wasteland Scavenger, it gets +2/+0 until end of turn.

example_cards.name: Cybernetic Mutant
example_cards.oracle_text:
Jury-rig
Cybernetic Mutant gets +1/+1 for each Equipment jury-rigged to it.
{B}{G}: Regenerate Cybernetic Mutant.

example_cards.name: Fusion Prototype
example_cards.oracle_text:
When Fusion Prototype enters the battlefield, create two Scrap tokens. (They're colorless Equipment artifacts with equip {1}.)
Jury-rig costs {0}.
Whenever you jury-rig an Equipment, Fusion Prototype gets +2/+2 and gains trample until end of turn.
